shape.description: string (the spot description shown to the player)
shape.board: string | null (community cards as a notation like '7h 8c Ts')
shape.available_actions: string[] (length >= 2; the choices the player can pick)

shape.action: string (must be one of the scenario's available_actions)

shape.scenario_seed: string (RNG seed used to deterministically pick per-round scenario sets)

shape.participants: Array<{ uid: string, displayName: string, joinedAt: ISO 8601 }> (length 1..2)
shape.rounds: Array<{ roundIndex: integer, leaderUid: string, scenarioIds: string[], submissionsByUid: Record<uid, PlayerSubmission[]> }>

exports.name: sampleNScenarios
exports.signature: (n: integer, rngSeed: string) -> Scenario[]; deterministic given rngSeed

invariants: Type shapes are stable across builders working on either side; any change requires an interface-revision step.
sampleNScenarios is pure — same (n, rngSeed) inputs always return the same Scenario[] in the same order.
